pattern: 'Good' numbers -- digits strictly increasing or strictly decreasing -- are so rare that every one of them can simply be enumerated once from digit subsets rather than discovered by scanning; counting how many other numbers merely have a good digit sum then becomes a small digit-DP over digit position, tight-bound, and running sum, combined with inclusion-exclusion so numbers that are good for both reasons are not counted twice.
hints: There are only a few thousand 'good' numbers up to the constraint ceiling -- generate them directly by choosing a subset of digits and arranging it strictly ascending (digits 1-9) or strictly descending (digits 0-9), rather than testing every integer in the range.

A number's digit sum is at most 9 times its digit count, so the set of digit sums that are themselves 'good' is tiny -- reuse the same generated set, filtered to small values, to know which sums qualify.

Counting how many integers up to some bound N have a digit sum in a target set is a classic digit-DP: recurse over digit positions carrying whether you are still bound by N's own digits and the running digit-sum so far.

Combine the two ways a number can be fancy with inclusion-exclusion: (good numbers in range) + (numbers in range whose digit sum is good) - (numbers in range that are both), since the small good-number set makes checking 'both' a direct lookup rather than a second DP.

edge_inputs.description: small range mixing single digits and one two-digit decreasing number
adjacent five-digit numbers, only one has a good digit sum
single nine-digit number that is neither good nor has a good digit sum
lower boundary of the constraint range
a window straddling a power-of-ten boundary where none of the numbers are monotone themselves, yet every one is fancy through a good digit sum
a run of consecutive digit sums that are mostly good, plus one number that is monotone in its own right despite an un-good digit sum -- exercises the good/digit-sum overlap without double counting
narrow window at the very top of the constraint range, near 10^15
window straddling a large power of ten so both good numbers and digit-sum matches must be recognized at full scale

stress.description: narrow random windows anchored at large values across the full 10^15 range, keeping brute-force cross-checking tractable while still exercising the digit-DP at extreme digit counts
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    width = min(size, 500)
    l = rng.randint(1, 1000000000000000 - width)
    r = l + width
    return [l, r]

stress.description: small-magnitude ranges where direct enumeration is feasible for an easy sanity sweep
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    l = rng.randint(1, 2000000 - size)
    r = l + rng.randint(0, size)
    return [l, r]
